title: Hook Execution Contract v1
description: Execution semantics contract for all hook runners in the ecosystem. Codifies the fail-open invariant: hooks must never block host commands on timeout, non-zero exit, or crash. Producer: any hook runner (cortina, volva, stipe). Consumer: stipe doctor, cortina hook runner, lamella hook templates.

hook_id.description: Stable identifier for this hook entry. Used for logging, doctor output, and deduplication.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum allowed wall-clock time in milliseconds before the hook process is killed. Must be between 1 and 30000. Default is 10000. Values above 30000 are rejected by stipe doctor because they risk hanging user commands.

on_timeout.description: Required behavior when the hook exceeds timeout_ms. Must always be 'proceed': kill the hook process, emit a warning log, and allow the host command to continue. Blocking on timeout is never permitted.

on_error.description: Required behavior when the hook exits with a non-zero exit code or is killed by a signal. Must always be 'proceed': log a warning and allow the host command to continue. Blocking on non-zero exit is never permitted.

zero.description: Exit code 0 means the hook ran successfully.

non_zero.description: Any non-zero exit code is an advisory failure. It is logged as a warning but never causes the host command to fail.

exit_code_semantics.description: Defines the semantic meaning of hook exit codes. Non-zero exit codes are always advisory and never blocking.

stderr_disposition.description: Controls how hook stderr output is handled. 'log' captures stderr and emits it as a warning. 'suppress' discards stderr silently. In either case stderr is never forwarded to the user as an error.
